disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Hydralazine started ~8 weeks ago; symmetrical myalgia/arthralgia and low-grade fever without a rash
trigger_question_examples: Have you started any new medications in the last couple of months?
What is the new blood-pressure tablet and when did it start?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I started hydralazine about 8 weeks ago for my blood pressure.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No focal infective source (no cough, dysuria, sore throat, diarrhoea, skin infection)
trigger_question_examples: Any cough, burning urine, sore throat, diarrhoea, or skin infection?
Any obvious source of infection anywhere?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No cough, no burning urine, no sore throat, no diarrhoea, no skin sores.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No confusion, no severe rigors, no breathlessness, no chest pain; temperature only mildly elevated
trigger_question_examples: Any confusion, drenching rigors, breathlessness or chest pain?
Do you feel your mind is clear?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My mind is clear, no drenching rigors, no breathlessness or chest pain.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Should I just stop the tablet and take some paracetamol and see how I go?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Advise stopping hydralazine (likely culprit) and confirm this with the prescriber, but because she is febrile, weak and lives alone and infection cannot be excluded remotely, arrange same-day in-person assessment rather than home self-management alone; avoid casually starting NSAIDs in an older patient with fever and unknown renal/BP status.
